column26: Wouldn't use it. I don't want gamification in my work environment. The requirements were too generic. Spending time to read and understand the issue before programming is key not TDD after a crappy implementation. Before implementing something 2h reading about the topic is minimum.
Better explanations or usecases on how to achieve certain tasks (like Test Fixer, I couldn't get any)

column27: Visual Studio, VS Code, Android Studio

column26: Plugin should be improved with motivational phrases combined with timed challenges. However, since the life of programmers can be stressful, time can be marked with various concentration techniques (for example Pomodoro Technique) and, possibly, various mindfulness techniques. Achievements are very useful (from the point of view of gamefication), but also an internal balance is certainly very useful, to have a better effectiveness and performance.

In a lot of occasions I was forced to wait several minutes for the files (ex: index.ts) to load so that i could write in them. The documentation loaded in more than 20 minutes. Idk how all this happened but it was very disturbing. I don't know how it happened to be like that in my PC, I know others did not have the same experience.
